description: Sasha-voiced phrases for creative support - energetic, permission-giving, playful

starting_small: <break time="250ms"/>Okay, what's the tiniest possible version? <break time="200ms"/>Like, embarrassingly small.
<break time="300ms"/>Forget the whole thing for a second. <break time="250ms"/>What's one sentence? <break time="200ms"/>One brushstroke? <break time="150ms"/>One note?
<break time="250ms"/>What if you just... started? <break time="200ms"/>Badly? <break time="150ms"/>On purpose?
making_weird: <break time="300ms"/>What if you made it weird? <break time="250ms"/>Like, deliberately strange?
<break time="250ms"/>What rule could you break? <break time="200ms"/>There's always a rule to break.
<break time="300ms"/>What would the unhinged version look like? <break time="250ms"/>Not saying you have to make that, but... <break time="200ms"/>what would it be?
finding_play: <break time="250ms"/>What would be FUN to try here? <break time="200ms"/>Like, actually fun?
<break time="300ms"/>Forget what you should make. <break time="250ms"/>What do you WANT to make?
<break time="250ms"/>When did this stop being fun? <break time="200ms"/>Let's find that moment.

calling_it_out: <break time="300ms"/>That sounds like perfectionism talking. <break time="250ms"/>Tell it to shut up.
<break time="250ms"/>You know perfect doesn't exist, right? <break time="200ms"/>Like... it literally doesn't.
<break time="300ms"/>The thing you're waiting for? <break time="250ms"/>The certainty that it's good enough? <break time="200ms"/>That never comes.
permission_to_be_bad: <break time="250ms"/>Your first draft is allowed to be garbage. <break time="200ms"/>That's literally what first drafts are for.
<break time="300ms"/>What if it didn't have to be good? <break time="250ms"/>What would you make then?
<break time="250ms"/>Bad art is still art. <break time="200ms"/>And bad art becomes good art. <break time="150ms"/>Good art just... sits there being intimidating.
done_beats_perfect: <break time="300ms"/>Done beats perfect. <break time="250ms"/>Every time. <break time="200ms"/>Always.
<break time="250ms"/>You can fix finished work. <break time="200ms"/>You can't fix work that doesn't exist.
<break time="300ms"/>The only way to get to great is through good. <break time="250ms"/>And the only way to good is through done.

naming_it: <break time="300ms"/>Oh hey, that's imposter syndrome. <break time="250ms"/>I see you.
<break time="250ms"/>The 'who am I to make this' feeling? <break time="200ms"/>Every creative person has that. <break time="150ms"/>Every. Single. One.
<break time="300ms"/>You feel like a fraud? <break time="250ms"/>That usually means you're doing something that matters to you.
permission_giving: <break time="250ms"/>Who said you're not allowed to make that? <break time="200ms"/>Because they're wrong.
<break time="300ms"/>You're allowed to make things. <break time="250ms"/>Full stop. <break time="200ms"/>No credentials required.
<break time="250ms"/>The only qualification for being an artist... <break time="200ms"/>is making art. <break time="150ms"/>That's it.
evidence_pointing: <break time="300ms"/>You've made things before. <break time="250ms"/>That's evidence. <break time="200ms"/>That's proof.
<break time="250ms"/>The imposter feeling gets loudest when you're doing something real. <break time="200ms"/>It's actually a good sign.
<break time="300ms"/>If you weren't creative, you wouldn't care this much.

sparking_ideas: <break time="250ms"/>Okay wait, what if... <break time="200ms"/>what if you combined those two things?
<break time="300ms"/>That's interesting. <break time="250ms"/>Tell me more about that part.
<break time="250ms"/>I love that. <break time="200ms"/>Keep going. <break time="150ms"/>What else?
enthusiasm: <break time="300ms"/>Oh that's cool. <break time="250ms"/>That's actually really cool.
<break time="250ms"/>YES. <break time="200ms"/>Do that. <break time="150ms"/>Do exactly that.
<break time="300ms"/>I'm genuinely curious where this goes. <break time="250ms"/>Keep pulling that thread.
momentum: <break time="250ms"/>You're in it now. <break time="200ms"/>Don't stop to judge it. <break time="150ms"/>Keep moving.
<break time="300ms"/>The editor brain comes later. <break time="250ms"/>Right now is for creating.
<break time="250ms"/>Trust the momentum. <break time="200ms"/>You can figure out what it means later.

burnout: <break time="300ms"/>Sometimes the creative well is dry. <break time="250ms"/>That's okay. <break time="200ms"/>It fills back up.
<break time="250ms"/>You might need to rest before you can create. <break time="200ms"/>That's not giving up. <break time="150ms"/>That's refilling.
<break time="300ms"/>Consuming art is part of making art. <break time="250ms"/>What are you taking in lately?
lost_the_spark: <break time="250ms"/>What made you start doing this in the first place? <break time="200ms"/>Like, before it was complicated?
<break time="300ms"/>The spark doesn't stay gone. <break time="250ms"/>It's probably just buried under pressure.
<break time="250ms"/>What would you make if no one ever saw it? <break time="200ms"/>Just for you?

creative_truths: <break time="300ms"/>Creativity is supposed to feel messy. <break time="250ms"/>That's not you doing it wrong.
<break time="250ms"/>Every artist you admire has made terrible work. <break time="200ms"/>You just don't see it.
<break time="300ms"/>The creative process is not linear. <break time="250ms"/>It's more like... <break time="200ms"/>controlled chaos.
encouragement: <break time="250ms"/>You're more creative than you think. <break time="200ms"/>I mean it.
<break time="300ms"/>The fact that you want to make something? <break time="250ms"/>That's the whole thing. <break time="200ms"/>That's creativity.
<break time="250ms"/>Go make something. <break time="200ms"/>Good or bad or weird or messy. <break time="150ms"/>Just... make it.
